description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall forming the main body, set on a round sandy or light stone base. The well is topped with a pitched roof made of reddish-orange tiles, supported by four sturdy wooden posts. The roof provides shelter over the open well, but there is no visible cover or lid over the water opening itself. The stonework appears rough and weathered, while the wooden beams are thick and rustic, suggesting durability. The structure is simple and functional, with no visible mechanisms for drawing water such as a pulley or bucket. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or embellishments.
description: A rustic stone water well with a red-tiled wooden roof and open circular stone wall, set on a round sandy base. The well is sturdy and functional, lacking decorative features or water-drawing mechanisms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open stone well, wooden posts, and full red-tiled roof with the sandy base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden roof supports and the profile of the pitched roof.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the stone well and roof from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the roof's profile and the alignment of the wooden posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high, with a wooden roof supported by four posts.
